sections.paragraph:
ἀμφιβολία, ας, ἡ (cp. ἀμφιβάλλω; Hdt. et al. in var. mngs.; Aristot.; SIG 827 Tit. F col. V, 6; PLond 1716, 8 [VI a.d.]; Jos., Bell. 3, 434; Ath. 11, 2, R. 60, 15) a quarrel ἀ. ἔχειν μετά τινος D 14:2. B. 1244.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

var. var. = various(ly)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

col. col. = column

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 55
